items.title: Trump Pulls Up To Turkey For High-Stakes Meeting With NATO Allies
items.desc: Trump is attending the 2026 NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, to meet with NATO allies.

items.title: US Revokes Waiver Allowing Iran Oil Sales After Attacks
items.desc: The US Treasury Department revoked a waiver that allowed the sale of Iranian oil in response to attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, jeopardizing an interim peace deal between Washington and Tehran.

items.title: Quick commerce slows down, riders face challenges amid Pune rains
items.desc: Monsoon rains in Pune are delaying quick commerce deliveries beyond 10–20 minutes, causing surge fees, paused orders, and rider safety and bike damage concerns.

items.desc: Heavy monsoon rain triggers flood-like conditions in Jabalpur division; Balaghat, Mandla and Dindori hit, rescues ongoing, roads cut off, IMD yellow alert.

items.title: Friend or enemy? China missile test sees Pacific nations finally speak out
items.desc: This week, perhaps for the first time in a long time, some sections of the Pacific are signalling a slight adjustment to their relationship with China.

items.title: Police to deploy bandobast for palkhi procession, use AI tools, GPS & drones to monitor crowds
items.desc: Pune police to deploy 7,000 staff for palkhi processions July 9-11, using AI analytics, GPS, drones and FRS CCTV; bomb squad, checkpoints, traffic curbs.

items.title: Even after schedule trolling, UT's Sarkisian comforts Texas Tech coach
items.desc: Even after Texas Tech trolled UT begging Texas to play in two months, Steve Sarkisian still reached out to Joey McGuire to comfort him in dark time.

items.title: US Allegations: Iran Fired Missiles At Vessels In Strait of Hormuz
items.desc: The United States ruling class is alleging that Iran fired missiles at a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Monday night. These strikes threaten to destabilize the already shaky memorandum of understanding signed by both the US and Iranian presidents.
